NVDA.title: Q3 earnings will be 'validation moment' for AI spend: Dan Ives
NVDA.summary: Big Tech heavy hitters will report their third quarter earnings in the coming October weeks, which will give Wall Street a better sense of how much these companies are investing in AI and artificial intelligence infrastructures. Wedbush Securities managing director and global head of technology research Dan Ives discusses how investors are still underestimating the AI super cycle propelling markets (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) higher. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

NVDA.title: Shutdown, China cracks down on Nvidia, Oil prices
NVDA.summary: The US Government shutdown continues to rumble on with little sign of letting up, China begins cracking down on Nvidia (NVDA) chips being imported into the country and Oil (CL=F) prices stumble after peace appears to be agreed in the Middle East.

TSLA.title: Trump Fires Federal Workers, Escalating US Shutdown Fight
TSLA.summary: “It’ll be a lot, and we’ll announce the numbers over the next couple of days, but it’ll be a lot of people,” Trump told reporters Friday in the Oval Office.  The administration plans to slash at least 4,100 workers from the government during the shutdown, according to newly filed court documents.

TSLA.title: The Score: Delta, AMD, Tesla and More Stocks That Defined the Week
TSLA.summary: Sunny quarterly results from  Delta Air Lines  sent air carrier stocks soaring on Thursday.  Delta said it expects travel demand to continue to bounce back and is aiming for a record holiday season.  Delta shares rose 4.3% Thursday.
